macro_segments.title: Reading Torah on Rosh Chodesh Tevet When It Falls on Shabbat: The Conflict Between Chanukah and Rosh Chodesh

micro_segments.title: The case of Rosh Chodesh Tevet falling on Shabbat and the need for multiple Torah scrolls
Rav Yitzchak Naphcha's position: three aliyot for Rosh Chodesh, one for Chanukah
Rav Dimi miHaifa's contrary view: three for Chanukah, one for Rosh Chodesh
The principle of tadir (more frequent) taking precedence and the ruling against it
Rav Yosef and Rava's dispute: whether to weight Rosh Chodesh or Chanukah; the final halakha

macro_segments.title: The Challenge of Parshat Shekalim When It Coincides With Parshat Ki Tissa

micro_segments.title: When Parshat Shekalim precedes Ki Tissa: Rav Yitzchak Naphcha's approach of reading through Ki Tissa
Abaye's objection and his solution: reading six aliyot to Vayakhel, then repeating for clarity
The teaching (tanya) that supports Abaye's approach of reading and repeating
When Parshat Shekalim falls on Ki Tissa itself: whether to repeat the opening section

macro_segments.title: The Calendar of Special Parshiyot and Rosh Chodesh Adar: Determining Which Shabbat Reads Shekalim

micro_segments.title: When Rosh Chodesh Adar falls in the middle of the week: moving Shekalim to the previous Shabbat
The dispute between Rav and Shmuel over Erev Shabbat: advancing versus postponing
The source from the Baraita and the reading of 'be-tocha' (during) versus 'bah' (on)
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar's position: no skipping when Rosh Chodesh falls Erev Shabbat

macro_segments.title: The Dispute Over Parshat Zachor When Purim Falls on Erev Shabbat

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: advance Zachor when Purim falls on Friday
Shmuel's position: postpone Zachor to the day after Purim on Friday
Rav's reasoning: the obligation to remember must precede the action (achrayat Amalek)
Shmuel's counter: those who observe Purim on the 15th (mukafinor) have action and remembrance together
Proof from the Baraita: when Purim falls during a week, Zachor is read that Shabbat
When Purim itself falls on Shabbat: Rav Huna's statement that even then Zachor is advanced

macro_segments.title: Parshat Parah and the Reference Point of Rosh Chodesh Nissan

micro_segments.title: The definition of the third special Shabbat: the Shabbat following Purim or preceding Rosh Chodesh Nissan
Rav Chama bar Chanina's clarification: Parah is read on the Shabbat immediately before Rosh Chodesh Nissan
Reconciling the definitions: different scenarios depending on when Rosh Chodesh Nissan falls

macro_segments.title: The Four Special Parshiyot and Their Shabbatot: The Complete Baraita and Rashi's Calendar System

micro_segments.title: The Baraita listing all four special Shabbatot: Shekalim, Zachor, Parah, and Hachodesh with their dates and haftarot
Rashi's mnemonic (Zavadu, Baro, Gad, V'haya) for determining which Shabbatot to skip based on when Rosh Chodesh Adar falls
Three scenarios: Rosh Chodesh on Shabbat, Monday, Wednesday, or Erev Shabbat and the resulting calendar of readings

macro_segments.title: The Four Special Parshiyot and Their Shabbatot: The Complete Baraita and Rashi's Calendar System (continued)

micro_segments.title: The note on writing the 15th as yud-heh and avoiding the divine name in numerical form

macro_segments.title: Resuming Regular Parshiyot and the Debate Over Whether to Interrupt the Torah Reading or the Haftarah

micro_segments.title: On the fifth Shabbat, returning to the normal order of parshiyot
Rav Ami vs. Rabbi Yirmiyah: does one interrupt the Torah reading or only the haftarah?
Resolving the dispute: when dealing with weekday readings (ta'aniyot, ma'amadot), the Torah reading is interrupted; on Shabbat, only the haftarah changes

macro_segments.title: Special Torah Readings on Festivals and Public Days

micro_segments.title: Readings for Pesach, Shavuot, Rosh Hashanah, Yom Kippur, and Sukkot from the Mishnah
Chanukah, Purim, Rosh Chodesh, ma'amadot, and ta'aniyot readings
The rule against splitting the curse section (klalot) into multiple aliyot on ta'aniyot
Monday, Thursday, and Mincha on Shabbat readings do not count toward the following Shabbat's division
The source: Vayidaber Moshe et Moadei Hashem—readings must correspond to their proper times
